zh.lifeSummary: 當Reuben Allen於1698年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托達特姆斯時，他的父親Ralph Allen Sr82歲，母親Susanna (Unknown)79歲。他於大約1720年與Mary Jackson在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加马里兰結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1741年11月26日在美國維吉尼亞州申南多阿莫特 傑克遜享年43歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州普林斯 威廉申南多阿。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Reuben Allen于1698年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托達特姆斯时，他的父亲Ralph Allen Sr82岁，母亲Susanna (Unknown)79岁。他于大约1720年与Mary Jackson在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加马里兰结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1741年11月26日在美国弗吉尼亚州申南多阿莫特 傑克遜享年43岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州普林斯 威廉申南多阿。

nameMeanings.name: Allen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Allenby
meanings: English: habitational name from Allonby (Cumberland). The placename is a post-Conquest compound of the French (Breton) personal name Alein (see Allen ) + Old Norse bȳ ‘village’, or the same word borrowed into Middle English.

variants.name: Allin
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Allen  .
Swedish: variant of   Ahlin  .

variants.name: Alano
meanings: Italian (mainly Campania) and Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from the personal name Alano (see Allen ).

variants.name: Alan
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Allan
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Allen  . This is the more common form of the name in Scotland and northern England. See also   McAllen  .

History: An early bearer of this surname in North America was William Allan, a British soldier who took his family to the newly founded city of Halifax, NS, Canada, in 1749. His son John (1746–1805) was a Revolutionary soldier who eventually (1783) founded the settlement of Allan's Island, Passamaquoddy Bay, ME.

variants.name: Allain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Paul, Adelard, Alban, Albenie, Alphonse, Andree, Cyrille, Donat, Emile, Euclide, Francois.

Breton and French: from the Old Breton personal name Alan (Old French Alain); see Allen . In France the personal name is spelled Alain, and this is also a less common variant of the surname (see Alain ).
English: variant of   Allen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Alen
meanings: English: variant of   Allen  .
Galician (Alén): habitational name from one of the many places called Alén in Galicia (Spain), from Galician alén ‘beyond’.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from   Ale  .

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + haid ‘‘rank, worth’’.

variants.name: Alleyne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Chantal, Julien, Marcell.

English: variant of   Allen  . This is an old-fashioned spelling, already established as a surname in England in Tudor times. Today this surname is found mainly in the West Indies, especially Barbados.

variants.name: Alling
meanings: English: either a variant of Allen , or a variant of Halling with loss of initial H-.
German: habitational name from either of two places called Alling, one in Bavaria and one in Austria.
Danish: habitational name from any of several places called Alling. The placename may be a derivative of al ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: Reuben
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (said to mean ‘behold, a son’ in Hebrew), borne by one of the twelve sons of Jacob, and so the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel. Genesis 29:32 explains it as follows: ‘and Leah conceived, and bare a son, and she called his name Reuben: for she said, Surely the Lord hath looked upon my affliction; now therefore my husband will love me’. In Genesis 30:14–15, Reuben is depicted as a devoted son to his mother, but he incurred his father's wrath for seducing his concubine Bilhah and on his deathbed Jacob, rather than blessing him, cursed Reuben because of this incident (Genesis 49:4). Despite this, the name has enjoyed steady popularity as a Jewish name. Among Christians (chiefly Nonconformists) it came into use after the Reformation, survived into the 20th century, and is currently enjoying a modest revival.

variants.name: Reuven
meanings: Biblical name (said to mean ‘behold, a son’ in Hebrew), borne by one of the twelve sons of Jacob, and so the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel. Genesis 29:32 explains it as follows: ‘and Leah conceived, and bare a son, and she called his name Reuben: for she said, Surely the Lord hath looked upon my affliction; now therefore my husband will love me’. In Genesis 30:14–15, Reuben is depicted as a devoted son to his mother, but he incurred his father's wrath for seducing his concubine Bilhah and on his deathbed Jacob, rather than blessing him, cursed Reuben because of this incident (Genesis 49:4). Despite this, the name has enjoyed steady popularity as a Jewish name. Among Christians (chiefly Nonconformists) it came into use after the Reformation, survived into the 20th century, and is currently enjoying a modest revival.

en.lifeSummary: When Reuben Allen was born in 1698, in Dartmouth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, his father, Ralph Allen Sr, was 82 and his mother, Susanna (Unknown), was 79. He married Mary Jackson about 1720, in Maryland, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 26 November 1741, in Mount Jackson, Shenandoah, Virginia, United States, at the age of 43, and was buried in Shenandoah, Prince William, Virginia, British Colonial America.

Il a épousé Mary Jackson environ 1720, à Maryland, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 26 novembre 1741, à Mount Jackson, Shenandoah, Virginie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Shenandoah, Prince William, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Reuben Allen è nato il nel 1698 nel Dartmouth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Ralph Allen Sr, aveva 82 anni e il suo madre, Susanna (Unknown), aveva 79 anni. Lui sposato Mary Jackson circa 1720, nel Maryland, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 26 novembre 1741, nel Mount Jackson, Shenandoah, Virginia, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Shenandoah, Prince William, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Reuben Allenが1698年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーブリストルダルトモウスで生まれた時，父Ralph Allen Srは82歳，母Susanna (Unknown)は79歳であった。約1720年にイギリス植民地アメリカメリーランド州でMary Jacksonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1741年11月26日にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州シェナンドアモウント ジャクソンで43歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州プリンス ウィリアムシェナンドアにて埋葬された。

ko.Shenandoah, Prince William, Virginia, Br: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 프린스 윌리엄 셰난도

ko.lifeSummary: Reuben Allen(이)가 1698년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 브리스톨 더르트모우스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph Allen Sr(은)는 82세였고, 그의 어머니 Susanna (Unknown)(은)는 79세였다. 그는 대략 1720년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 메릴랜드 주에서 Mary Jackson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1741년 11월 26일 미국 버지니아 주 셰난도 모우트 잭슨에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 프린스 윌리엄 셰난도에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Reuben Allen nasceu em 1698, em Dartmouth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, Ralph Allen Sr, tinha 82 anos e sua mãe, Susanna (Unknown), tinha 79 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Jackson aproximadamente 1720, em Maryland, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 26 de novembro de 1741, em Mount Jackson, Shenandoah, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Shenandoah, Prince William, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.near Mount Jackson, Shenandoah, Virginia: маунт джекксон, шенандоах, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Reuben Allen родился в 1698 г. в местности дартмоут, бристол, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Ralph Allen Sr было 82 года, а матери, которую звали Susanna (Unknown), 79. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Jackson около 1720 г. в местности Мэриленд, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 26 ноября 1741 г. в местности маунт джекксон, шенандоах, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности шенандоах, принц уиллам, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

Se casó con Mary Jackson aproximadamente 1720, en Maryland, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 26 de noviembre de 1741, en Mount Jackson, Shenandoah, Virginia, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Shenandoah, Prince William, Virginia, British Colonial America.
